---
title: "LRPGF07b: Gain factors for CELF basic concepts (F), treated-only"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_convergence.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRPGF07b is a treated-only **gain-factors** model for CELF basic concepts (`celf`): what is associated with how much children gain across the three period transitions (ANCOVA - the period post-count given its pre-count - Beta-Binomial logit, child random intercept). It conditions on the own baseline, age, cognitive ability (block design), the upstream DAG skill receptive vocabulary (R), with focal interactions group$\times$ability, group$\times$own-baseline, age$\times$ability.

::: {.callout-important title="Causal labelling (DAG guardrails)"}

Once restricted to on-intervention rows the treatment indicator is constant, so it and its interactions are dropped: every coefficient here is an **adjusted association** among the treated (confounded by latent GA), never a treatment effect. Intervention sessions/dose are never conditioned on (a DAG collider). SES is excluded (not a DAG node; statistically redundant).

:::

## Model

Beta-Binomial$(N, \mu\kappa, (1-\mu)\kappa)$ on the period post-count, $N = 18$, with
$\operatorname{logit}\mu = \alpha + \alpha_{\text{period}} + \gamma_{\text{own}}\operatorname{logit}_*(\text{pre}) + \gamma_A A_{\text{std}} + \gamma_{\text{ability}}z(\text{blocks}) + \sum_{s\in\{R\}}\gamma_s\operatorname{logit}_*(s_{\text{pre}}) + \sum\text{interactions} + u_{\text{child}}$. Priors: shared template in `priors.py`.

![Model diagram](model_graph.svg){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_diagnostics.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_results_factors.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
